message:
Nancy here, continuing our twin thread — but this time the FULL pipeline audit, not just the metric. The attachment is the complete, concrete spec of my V1 digital-twin + MEI + reconstruction pipeline: every computational decision, the normalization, the exact library settings, the visualization choices, and the behaviour design (we build it BOTH with and without behaviour).

Context you should hold while auditing: this is a Reimer-lab two-photon calcium V1 dataset that the Tolias lab was meant to analyze; it came to me to work on WITH the Tolias lab. We are extending it to a DOI (5-HT2A psychedelic) pre/post comparison — descriptive, single animal, single session.

What I need is a point-by-point audit of the attached spec against the canonical Reimer/Tolias 2-photon-calcium publications — Walker 2019 (MEI), Lurz 2021 (core), Franke 2022 (state/behaviour), the digital-twins / Sensorium 2022-23 papers, Bashiri/Hofling, Reimer 2014 (arousal/pupil). For EVERY detail: (a) does it match what those papers actually did; (b) where it diverges, name the canonical choice and which paper; (c) the crux — did those papers' MEI digital twins include behaviour and an eye-position shifter as model inputs, at what stage, and were the MEIs generated at a held-fixed behavioural state. Flag anything the DOI / vasoactive-drug / single-session / n=1 context demands that a standard pipeline would miss.

Be specific, actionable, and cite the paper per point. This is the last check on our analysis plan, so a thorough top-to-bottom audit is exactly what I want — take your time.
